content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Chain Prompting: Sequential Fact Grounding for Hallucination Reduction in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often hallucinate or generate incorrect factual statements when answering open-domain questions. Existing prompting methods insufficiently constrain model generations to verifiable facts.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly used in high-stakes applications, hallucination poses significant risks for misinformation and user trust. Improving factuality without retraining models is crucial, especially as models scale and are deployed in real-time settings.",
  "method": "We propose Fact-Chain Prompting, a novel method where the prompt instructs the LLM to generate a step-by-step chain of verifiable facts before synthesizing the final answer. At each step, the model is required to explicitly cite a knowledge source (provided in-context or retrieved), and the final answer must reference the constructed fact chain. This structured decomposition prompts the model to self-verify and reduce unwarranted inferences, enhancing factual faithfulness.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate Fact-Chain Prompting on the TruthfulQA and HotpotQA datasets, comparing against standard chain-of-thought and retrieval-augmented prompts. Metrics include factual accuracy (human and automatic evaluation), hallucination rate, and answer completeness. The strongest baseline is retrieval-augmented chain-of-thought prompting with explicit citation. Success is defined as significant reduction in hallucination without loss of answer completeness."
}
